dialogueChunks.intro: The doors opened this morning. Most walked out. Some didn't.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The partial solution worked better than {target_npc} expected. Not everyone could be saved, and the families of those who didn't make it aren't ready to call this a victory. But the council never had to make the worst call. {quest_ally} is already distributing the remaining remedy.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave us a choice that wasn't between terrible and worse, {player_name}. The losses are a tragedy. The alternative would have been an atrocity.

dialogueChunks.intro: All of them, {player_name}. Every captive. Walking through the gates right now.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} didn't expect a strike on both locations simultaneously. {target_npc} coordinated the timing while {quest_ally} led the second effort. It was close: closer than anyone wants to admit. But nobody was left behind. The stores are untouched.
dialogueChunks.outro: You found the third option, {player_name}. The one nobody believed existed. Families are whole tonight because you refused to accept the impossible choice.

dialogueChunks.intro: {quest_focus_The} {quest_focus_is} gone. It collapsed an hour after the last person crossed. The commander never had to give the order.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Everyone made it through the alternate path. The elder had to be carried the last mile, and one of the children fell asleep on a soldier's back. {target_npc} said the sound of {quest_focus_the} falling behind them was the worst and best thing they ever heard.
dialogueChunks.outro: You found the path nobody was looking for, {player_name}. {quest_stakes_The} survived because you wouldn't accept that saving the many meant abandoning the few.
